reasoning: The informal statement claims that null(T-λI) is invariant under S, where "invariant under S" in standard linear algebra (and specifically in Axler) means S(U) ⊆ U, not equality. The Lean formalization encodes this via `Submodule.map S (ker (T - c • LinearMap.id)) ≤ ker (T - c • LinearMap.id)`, which is precisely the ⊆ relation. The commutation `S ∘ T = T ∘ S` correctly captures ST = TS. The universal quantification over c : F corresponds to "for every λ ∈ F", and `ker (T - c • LinearMap.id)` is the null space of T - cI. The ground truth file itself demonstrates that using `=` would be incorrect (via an explicit counterexample) and provides the `≤` version as the correct formalization. Hence this is a faithful encoding of the informal claim.
